layman_headline: The market is fearful and all swing candidates look weak — patience is the play right now.
layman_explanation: Bitcoin has bounced modestly this week but the broader altcoin market remains in a downtrend. Every coin on our watchlist is either in a confirmed bearish chart pattern or has already run up too much to chase safely. The Fear & Greed index sits at 29 ('Fear'), which often precedes good buying opportunities — but we're not there yet. The best approach is to wait for a clearer reversal signal before committing capital.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market hasn't given a high-confidence entry yet. Watch Ethereum closely near $1,800 support — if it holds and RSI dips below 35, that could be the setup worth taking.
expert_reasoning: All nine swing candidates present unfavorable risk/reward for immediate entry. BONK has a strongly bearish chart bias (-2.0) with Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu — a classic falling knife. NIGHT is down 37% in 7 days with no volume data and no support levels identified. LDO has the best chart bias (+0.85) with Three White Soldiers, but RSI at 59 after a 23% weekly and 52% 5-day run makes chasing extremely risky with a poor 0.38x risk/reward ratio. ETH is the most compelling on a pullback — RSI at 39 (reliable signal per our credibility data at 80% win rate), $41B TVL providing fundamental floor, and the ETH derivatives reset narrative from Blockworks. However, price is below all MAs with a -0.70 chart bias, and BTC dominance at 56.6% continues to suppress alts. The prudent move is to wait for ETH to test $1,800 support and show a reversal candle before entering.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.6% continues rising, draining capital from altcoins — any position here fights the macro trend
All candidates are below key moving averages indicating persistent bearish structure across the alt market
Fear & Greed at 29 is improving but hasn't reached extreme fear levels (sub-20) that historically mark reliable bottoms
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a bounce with volume confirmation here would trigger a BUY
BTC ability to break and hold above $66,000 — would signal broader risk-on shift benefiting alts
LDO for a pullback toward $0.32-0.34 — the bullish chart structure could offer a better entry on a retest
market_context: The crypto market is in a 'Fear' state with BTC recovering modestly (+5.3% weekly) while maintaining 56.6% dominance. Altcoins remain under pressure with most candidates in confirmed downtrends, suggesting this is a patience phase rather than an action phase.
context_banner: Fearful market with all alt candidates in bearish chart structures — disciplined patience now sets up higher-conviction entries ahead.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal at 80% win rate — but needs price to reach support before acting on it.
Fear & Greed at 29 improving from weekly average of 25.9 suggests the worst panic may be fading, but hasn't reached extreme fear levels that mark strong bottoms.
Bitcoin defying tech stock selloff and eyeing $70K suggests the macro leader is strengthening, which could eventually lift alts — but hasn't yet.
ETH derivatives reset article suggests positioning has been cleaned out, creating conditions for a fresh directional move in Ethereum.
Every candidate except LDO carries a bearish chart bias (-0.35 to -2.0), and LDO's 0.38x risk/reward after a 52% 5-day surge makes chasing it too dangerous.
